messages.content:
The drug of choice for circulatory shock is …..

a.
Pseudoephedrine

b.
Noradrenaline

c.
Fenoldopam

d.
Dopamine
b. Noradrenaline

Which of the following drugs is used as antihypertensive?

a.
Ephedrine

b.
Amphetamine

c.
Dopamine

d.
Clonidine
d. Clonidine

Which of the following drugs is used in acute bronchial asthma?

a.
Salbutamol

b.
Ephedrine

c.
Salmetrol

d.
Formoterol
a. Salbutamol

A 22-year-old male is brought to the emergency room with suspected cocaine overdose. Which of the following symptoms is most likely in this patient?

a.
Bradycardia

b.
Hypertension

c.
Bronchoconstriction

d.
Miosis (constriction of pupil)

b. Hypertension

Cocaine overdose commonly presents with symptoms of increased heart rate, high blood pressure, and with possible symptoms of chest pain or heart rhythm abnormalities.

Which of the following adrenergic agonists is commonly present in nasal sprays available over-the-counter (OTC) to treat nasal congestion?

a.
Albuterol

b.
Clonidine

c.
Formoterol

d.
Oxymetazoline

d. Oxymetazoline

Oxymetazoline is a common adrenergic agonist found in OTC nasal sprays for nasal congestion.

A man with diabetic neuropathy complains of dizziness and fainting when arising from bed in the morning. Which drug would be most beneficial to this patient?

a.
Dobutamine

b.
Isoproterenol

c.
Midodrine

d.
Clonidine

c. Midodrine

Midodrine is a medication that is commonly used to treat orthostatic hypotension, which causes symptoms such as dizziness and fainting upon standing up. This medication can help increase blood pressure and alleviate symptoms in patients with conditions like diabetic neuropathy.

Which of the following adrenergic drugs is used in the treatment of overactive bladder?

a.
Dobutamine

b.
Phenylephrine

c.
Epinephrine

d.
Mirabegron

d. Mirabegron

Mirabegron is a selective β3-adrenergic receptor agonist used in the treatment of overactive bladder. It helps relax the bladder muscle to improve symptoms of urinary urgency, frequency, and incontinence.

Which of the following is an indirectly acting sympathomimetic amine?

a.
Tyramine

b.
Dopamine

c.
Isoprenaline

d.
Phenylephrine

a. Tyramine

Tyramine is an indirectly acting sympathomimetic amine that releases norepinephrine from sympathetic nerve terminals.

IV norepinephrine–induced bradycardia is mediated via:

a.
β1- adrenoceptors stimulation

b.
Reflex vagal stimulation

c.
α1-adrenoceptors stimulation

d.
Stimulation of Ca2+ channels

b. Reflex vagal stimulation

IV norepinephrine can induce bradycardia through reflex vagal stimulation as part of a baroreceptor reflex response to increased blood pressure caused by the drug's vasoconstrictive effects.

Β2 selective agonists are effective in treatment of

a.
Overactive bladder

b.
Hypertension

c.
Nasal congestion

d.
Asthma

d. Asthma

β2 selective agonists are commonly used in the treatment of asthma because they help relax the smooth muscles in the airways, leading to bronchodilation and the relief of asthma symptoms.
